titleStmt.title: Text corpus of the Projects A Comprehensive Edition of Tocharian Manuscripts and The Characters that Shaped the Silk Road

category.catDesc: ends do not touch base line
ends do not touch base line

p.note: only recto

publicationStmt.publisher: The Characters that shaped the Silk Road - A Database and Digital Paleography of Tarim

seg.seg: was cut off

p.note: Verso in Uigur.

The transcription/transliteration given here represents only the part of the content of this fragment that is discussed in
.

publicationStmt.publisher: The Characters that shaped the Silk Road - A Database and Digital Paleography of Tarim

history.provenance: found in Shorchuk Stadthöhleduring T III Š 72.01

w.n: “good, pleasant” “that which is good, a good being” “good, well” nom/acc.sg.m
“all, every, each, complete(ly), total(ly), everyone, all (people/things)” abl.sg/pl.m/f
“(uni.) much, many, very” “(adj.) many” “(n.) multitude, many” nom
“(uni.) much, many, very” “(adj.) many” “(n.) multitude, many” nom
“good, pleasant” “that which is good, a good being” “good, well” nom/acc.sg.m
“all, every, each, complete(ly), total(ly), everyone, all (people/things)” loc.sg/pl.m/f
“one who has accomplished all aims, name of Gautama Buddha” m.nom.sg
“Bodhisattva [a person who will become a Buddha]” m.nom.sg
“the island [i.e., continent] of Jambu (= Syzygium cumini) trees” continent of the terrestrial world in the cosmologies of Hinduism, Buddhism, and Jainism m.all.sg
Rākṣasa, a kind of harmful demon m.gen.pl
“cross, go beyond, violate, transgress, pass (time), pass away” “cause to be out of control” “to cross, (sur)pass, pass (time), commit (a sin)” gv.pret1.3sg.pst.act
“Sāgara, PN, name of a king” m.gen.sg
“Sāgara, PN, name of a king” m.acc.sg
“all, every, each, complete(ly), total(ly), everyone, all (people/things)” acc.sg.m
“the island [i.e., continent] of Jambu (= Syzygium cumini) trees” continent of the terrestrial world in the cosmologies of Hinduism, Buddhism, and Jainism m.gen.sg
“to cause to disappear, drive out, drive away” “to drive out, remove” kaus.pret2.3sg.pst.act
“(adj.) difficult, hard, troublesome” “(adv.) with difficulty” nom/acc.sg.m
“cross, go beyond, violate, transgress, pass (time), pass away” “cause to be out of control” “to cross, (sur)pass, pass (time), commit (a sin)” part.subj5.acc.sg.m
“cross, go beyond, violate, transgress, pass (time), pass away” “cause to be out of control” “to cross, (sur)pass, pass (time), commit (a sin)” gv.prs7.3pl.prs.act
“(subst.masc.sg) the three worlds” “(adj.) of the three worlds” nom/acc.sg

seg.seg: Der gute Ruf der Energischen verbreitet sich in den zehn Weltgegenden.
The good reputation of the energetic ones spreads to all corners of the world;
With energy, one will receive honour, appreciation, ovations and adulation from everyone,
one will soon overcome the enemies and soon attain prosperity. 1.
The energetic ones have many possessions, the energetic ones [also have] many followers,
to the energetic ones the enemies submit, to the energetic ones distinction comes,
the energetic ones [are] the protection of the beings, the energetic ones do not know fear.
Therefore energy is good and superior to all in my opinion. 2.
Due to his energy, then, in earlier times the son of the king Siddhārta, the Bodhisattva Sarvārthasiddha [“he succeeds in everything”] came down to the ocean

Durch seine Energie stieg da in früherer Zeit der Sohn des Königs Siddhārtha, der Bodhisattva Sarvārthasiddha, zum Ozean herab, ... ging sieben Tage lang bis zum Hals im Wasser, ... ging sieben Tage lang im Schlamm und ging, nachdem er sieben Tage lang im Wasser mit tausendblättrigen Lotusblumen von Lotus zu Lotus steigend (gegangen) war, fünf Meilen weit auf von Schlangen bedeckter Erde. ... Dann überschritt er die vierfachen, mit Schlangen bedeckten Festungsgräben [und] nachdem er [auch] die Netze mit vierfachen Fäden (?) aus Bergkristall(?) [und] die großen, furchtbaren Nāgas, die den Palast des Königs Sāgara bewachten, durchschritten hatte, erlangte er schließlich(?) vom König Sāgara das Cintāmaṇi-Juwel.
[and] went to the island of jewels.
(After having loaded? the ship) with pearls and [after having] dismissed the caravan to Jambudvīpa, during seven days he went up to the neck in water,
seven days he (swam?) with his arms,
during seven days he went over mud,
moving around for seven days, stepping from one lotus to another, in a water with lotuses with a thousand leaves five miles he went over the ground covered with snakes.
Then he went to the island of the Rākṣasas,
then to that of the Yakṣas, (then) to Baladvīpa.
Thereupon he crossed the moats covered with four kinds of snakes,

the four-fold nets, made by strings (?) of crystal, past the great [and] terrible Nāgas, who watched the palace of king Sāgara, he moved, and finally obtained as reward the cintāmaṇi-jewel from king Sāgara;
the poverty of the whole Jambudvīpa he made disappear.
Thus it is said: || In the Ṣ.-tune: || The energetic ones cross the ocean that is hard to traverse, The threefold world the good ones traverse due to energy.

p.note:
Transcription and translation are based on
.

Transcription and references have been transferred from the "Text and Reference Database of the Tocharian A Language" (Gerd Carling Lund University) (funded by the Bank of Sweden Tercentenary Foundation and SCAS).

The translation is largely based on
3-4 , and
.
This verse is spoken by Vīryavān, "the energetic one". The first line is lost.
The metre is 4x12 syllables, rhythm 5/7.

publicationStmt.publisher: The Characters that shaped the Silk Road - A Database and Digital Paleography of Tarim

history.provenance: found in Shorchuk Stadthöhleduring T III Š 72.02

w.n: “cross, go beyond, violate, transgress, pass (time), pass away” “cause to be out of control” “to cross, (sur)pass, pass (time), commit (a sin)” gv.prs7.3pl.prs.act
“good, pleasant” “that which is good, a good being” “good, well” nom.pl.m

“to eat, consume, eating, consuming, food” inf.n.subj5.nom/acc.sg

“good, pleasant” “that which is good, a good being” “good, well” nom/acc.sg.m
“being able to gather, assemble, congregate” part.subj5.nom.sg.m

“(tr.) destroy, lose” “(itr.) perish, disappear” “(act.) to destroy, lose” “(mid.) to fall into ruin, disappear” gv.prs10.3sg.prs.mid

“so, so much, also used as correlative pro-form” emph
“one, single” “plural: some, certain, the ones (opposed to others)” nom.sg.m
“(act. itr.) to come into being (itr.)” “(mid. tr.) to establish, create, accomplish (tr.)” kaus.prs8.3sg.prs.act.obj.3sg
“having been born, come into being” part.pret0.nom.sg.m

seg.seg: The threefold world the good ones traverse due to energy. Eventually the strong ones attain Buddhahood.
Not in the least must energy be decried.
There is no other thing that has turned out to the detriment of the whole world [circle of existences] as much as inertia [has].
For in earlier days the food of humans was rice, uncultivated and chaff-less,
and clothes made ready on wishing trees were theirs to put on.
[But] due to the collecting of rice by a listless person the wishing trees disappeared for them,
the cotton plants became visible for them [in place of the wishing trees]. The uncultivated, chaff-less rice [also] disappeared for them,
it [only] became visible through great labour (and with) the chaff.
Of listlessness, however, energy is the opposite,
therefore in my opinion, energy is superior to all (in the world).
Śilpavān [“the skillful one”] speaks: The skillfulness of humans is superior to all, because:
|| The good reputation of a craftsman is spread in region after region.
He worthy of being treated with reverence and worthy to gather the adulation of humans;
property, even if well kept, will inevitably be fully lost: water, fire, kings and thieves certainly cut off the hope/security (?),
Besitz, [auch] wenn er behütet wird, muss zugrunde gehen: Wasser, Feuer, Könige [und] Diebe zerstören die Sicherheit(?); aber die Kunstfertigkeit geht nicht verloren.
but the skill will never be lost.
and skill is the root of possession.
Anytime an artist creates a work of art he has five advantages [from it]. (cf 5)
And as often as an artist creates an art(istic) object, so often he has five advantages [from it].

Und wie oft/immer ein Kunstfertiger ein Kunstwerk zustande bringt (Med.), ebenso oft/immer werden ihm fünf Vorteile [daraus]: Der erste Vorteil ist für ihn das ausgeführte Werk, es kommt ihm zustande (Akt.). Der zweite [Vorteil] ...
One advantage to him is the object created,
it comes into existence. The second is the pleasure arisen from the skill; the third, he receives friendship from the beings; the fourth, [he obtains] students,

p.note: Transcription and references have been transferred from the "Text and Reference Database of the Tocharian A Language" (Gerd Carling Lund University) (funded by the Bank of Sweden Tercentenary Foundation and SCAS).

Transcription and translation are based on
.

The translation is largely based on
4-5 , and
.
The name of this metre (4x14, rhythm 7/7) is unknown.

publicationStmt.publisher: The Characters that shaped the Silk Road - A Database and Digital Paleography of Tarim

history.provenance: found in Shorchuk Stadthöhleduring T III Š 72.03

“truly, surely, indeed, in fact” emphatic particle

complementizer occuring in relative clauses (including adverbial clauses)
an epithet of the Buddha “god” “(voc.) [your] majesty” m/f.gen.pl

seg.seg: (the fourth [is, he obtains] students), and the fifth is for him the advantage of obtaining property beyond [that].
Of the household and of the gods' world, skill is the root.
Therefore it has been said by great teachers: || In the Ś.-Tune:||
Fertigkeit soll man zuerst erlangen, dann soll man Besitztümer sammeln.
[It is] Skill [that] one must acquire first,
then one must gather possessions; the gathered possessions, however, one must always divided into four parts:
one part shall be enjoyed in the house,

Die gesammelten Besitztümer aber soll man stets viermal teilen: Einen Teil soll man im Haus[halt] genießen, mit zweien soll man Gewerbe betreiben [und] mit Rücksicht auf spätere Not[zeiten] [wtl. für später Not erblickt habend] soll man den vierten gut bewahren.
with two an activity/trade shall be pursued;
with regard to times of need, one should well preserve the fourth one.
One activity is (providing) water to the earth, a second is tending the livestock, a third one is trade, a fourth one is increasing the livestock, a fifth --
--, a sixth is conservation.
Besitztümer, die so gesammelt werden, wachsen bei Tag [und] Nacht wie ...
Possessions that are gathered in that way by day and night increase like ...
or like ... [but] one shall not keep possession for old age, not for those standing above the land,
not for the cruel and evil ones,
and neither for the powerful ones. At all times he shall do only the thing one should do,
Man tue immer nur das, was man tun soll, nicht tue man das, was man nicht tun soll: Denn der Mensch, der das tut, was man tun soll, leuchtet wie glühendes Feuer.
never let him do the thing one should not do;
for the being who does the thing one should do shines like a glowing fire.
By his own relatives and friends ... received in the beginning [and] at the end, he roars in the midst of all his followers, confident as a bull [in the midst of his herd].
After having given and received gift[s], as it is proper, a being [is] equal to all: if it comes in the reach of death, it attains the dwelling of the gods (through its flawless perfection).

[After] having given and received gift[s], as it is proper, a being [is] equal to all: if it comes in the reach of death, it attains the dwelling of the gods through its flawless perfection. (cf 7; 241)

p.note:
Transcription and translation are based on
.

Transcription and references have been transferred from the "Text and Reference Database of the Tocharian A Language" (Gerd Carling Lund University) (funded by the Bank of Sweden Tercentenary Foundation and SCAS).

The translation is largely based on
5-7 , and
.
The metre of the following tune is 4x14, rhythm 7/7.
39 suggests a restoration tā(wi)trä instead of the traditionally supplemented form tā(ṣi)trä.

complementizer occuring in relative clauses (including adverbial clauses)

seg.seg: (it attains the dwelling of the gods) through its flawless perfection. Therefore in my opinion, human skill is superior to all. Prajñāvān [“the Wise”] speaks:
There is no other thing that does so much good to the world as wisdom,
because ignorance is the root of the spread of all bad things and all damages.
Wisdom, however, cuts off the root of foolishness.
Wherever on earth it strikes root, from there on its wood grows on earth for krośas. (cf 7)
Like that there is a plant named Krośavatī, in whatever earth it takes root, from there one krośa its stem will grow away far over the earth.
The krośa-plant is five hundred fathoms in size. Having grown one krośa, it is cut down.
(Undestroyed) it produces a new sprout. From there it grows again one krośa,
and undestroyed from there one krośa it grows.
Auf solche Art und Weise wachsend, verbreitet es sich bis zu 100 Meilen weit auf der Erde fort.
Growing in this way it spreads up to 100 miles in the earth.
|| In the K.-tune: ||
Even cut, even destroyed, the Krośavatī, with only a small piece left over, grows as much [as before].
Just so are the ignorant ones: even beaten, even nipped, as soon as they have the possibility, they spread in badness.
On the mountain Himavant, however, there is a king [named] Vajramukha [“Diamond-mouth”].
Wherever he bites it through, the Krośavatī plant is ruined completely. (cf 7)
Where this one bites it, the plant Krośavatī dies completely.
Just so are the beings because of their ignorance.
In all bad things and in all damages they spread.
Well, when the foolishness (Krośavatī is bitten through) by the wisdom Vajramukha
Well, when the foolishness Krośavatī is bitten through by the wisdom Vajramukha, at that moment the ruin of all harms is there, and the spread of all advantages. (cf 8)

.

7-8 , and
.
The metre of the tune is 4x12, rhythm 4/4/4.

“so, so much, also used as correlative pro-form”

seg.seg: (Well, if the foolishness) Krośavatī is bitten through (by the wisdom Vajramukha), at that moment the ruin of all harms is there,
and the spread of all advantages.
Therefore human wisdom is superior to all, since it is the root of all excellent qualities.
Even if a being is provided with a [beautiful] figure [and] lovely to look at, [but] it has no wisdom, then it looks exactly as if it were a fashioned or painted figure. (cf 8)
Ist auch ein Mensch mit Schönheit versehen [und] lieblich anzusehen, nur die Klugheit ist ihm nicht, [so] sieht er genau so [schön] aus, als ob er eine geformte oder gemalte Gestalt wäre.
Even if a being is provided with a [beautiful] figure [and] lovely to look at, [but] it has no wisdom, then it looks exactly as if it were a fashioned or painted figure.
It can inspire love, but it cannot create advantages.
Just like at another time some other painter went as a guest to the house of a mechanic.
The mechanic then treated the painter in every way with reverence, he covered a bed for him in a separate place in his house, (gave) him sesame oil
and set an artificial girl at the head [of his bed].
She, that with his [the mechanic’s] reverence held in her hand, as it were, beauty and reverence, attended to him.
But how so? || In the S.-tune:||
Like one ashamed casting her glance to the ground a little, she looked lovely;
as if ashamed she did not say a word and she did not laugh,
but as with love she stretched her arm as she attended [to him].
She drove away the burning from the entire body of the painter.
And the painter, who because of his ignorance took the wooden girl for a real one, thinks only to himself:

Darauf denkt der Maler, der infolge [seiner] Dummheit das hölzerne Mädchen für ein wirkliches Mädchen hielt, eben bei sich: "Ach diese Schönheit, ach diese Verschämtheit durch weibliche Schüchternheit! ..."
ah, such beauty,
ah, such bashfulness caused by female shyness! || In the S.-tune: || Does she not very much look like a Kāminī,

w.n: “then, so (with strengthening effect)” particle occurring predominantly in questions

“tax, tribute” “hand, paw” m.nom/acc.sg OR “in extension, at length”
“truly, surely, indeed, in fact” emphatic particle
“be confused, faint” “to fail, miss, to be confused” “to confuse, lead astray” inf.n.prs8.nom/acc.sg
complementizer occuring in relative clauses (including adverbial clauses)
“to see, look” part.prs5.nom.sg.m OR “vision, seeing” abstr.n.prs5.nom/acc.sg
“member of the warrior class, warrior” m.gen.sg
“(uni.) much, many, very” “(adj.) many” “(n.) multitude, many” gen

seg.seg: [But] anytime she stretches her arm when she serves me, she pulls [me] onto her lap, as it were. (cf 9; 258)
[Wenn] sie den Arm reckt, indem sie [mich] bedient, zieht [wtl. nimmt] sie [mich] gleichsam in [ihren] Schoß.
the noble one does not care about my conduct either. [But] anytime she stretches her arm when she serves me, she pulls [me] onto her lap, as it were. The jewelry upon her breasts hardly moves,
Das Geschmeide auf ihren Brüsten bewegt sich kaum, es hebt sich eben nur.
it merely is lifted a little.
Aber [wenn sie] auch schüchtern [ist], [so] macht sie doch mein Herz von Liebe froh.
But even though she is shy, she nevertheless gladdens my heart with love.
Who may she be? Is she the sister, the daughter, or the wife, or the servant of the mechanic, or has she come to him as a guest, just like me? (cf 9; 46)
Wer [ist] sie wohl? Ist sie des Mechanikers Schwester [oder] Tochter oder Frau oder Dienerin?
Who may she be?
Is she the sister, the daughter, or the wife, or the servant of the mechanic, or has she come to him as a guest, just like me?
(But) a guest would not have been charged to pay honour to [another] guest. (cf 9; 258)
(But) a guest would not have been charged to pay honour to [another] guest.
But very much the mechanic has shown his trust in me that he has left a girl so beautiful alone with me.
Darauf [denkt] der Maler: "Durch (meine) Leidenschaft soll (sie nicht geschädigt(?)) sein", er gähnt [und] macht Miene, die Hand zu strecken.
And the painter [says to himself]: By my passion she shall (not be aggrieved(?));
he yawns and is about to stretch out his hand.
Thus (he thinks) to himself while looking at the girl.
|| In the A.-tune: || But no! Love has arrived in person, in order to confuse me by his own face ...
What if perhaps she had seated herself right before [lit. manifestly] me to honour me?

What if perhaps she had seated herself right before [lit. manifestly] me to honour me? As long as I don't speak to [lit. honour] her with heart-felt affection [lit. honour], my dream [that] this gentle one serves me, does not get spoilt yet.

Wenn sie offenbar etwa nur, [um] mich zu verehren, vor mir sitzen (sollte), [so] stört diese Zarte, [mich] bedienend, [von sich aus] auch nicht meinen Traum, solange ich zu dieser nicht die Sprache des Herzens spreche.
As long as I don’t speak to [lit. honour] her with heart-felt affection [lit. honour], my dream [that] this gentle one serves me, does not get spoilt yet.
Again he thinks:
Wiederum denkt er: "Im Hinblick auf große Gefahr ist ja hier Klugen nicht erlaubt, zu zehnerlei Frauen Liebe zu äußern ..."
Seeing the great danger, the wise ones are not allowed here to profess love to ten kinds of women.
Thus it is said:

to the royal spouse, to the father’s spouse, to the spouse of a general, to that of a relative, to that of the teacher, to an exceedingly adulatory woman, to a woman thinking of profit, to a woman available to many, and mainly (to a beautiful)

p.note: Transcription and references have been transferred from the "Text and Reference Database of the Tocharian A Language" (Gerd Carling Lund University) (funded by the Bank of Sweden Tercentenary Foundation and SCAS).

Transcription and translation are based on
.

The translation is largely based on
9-10 , and
.

The stretching out of the painter's hand is most likely a gesture of dismissal; cf.
9, fn. 8 .
the tune is 4x14 syllables, rhythm 7/7.

publicationStmt.publisher: The Characters that shaped the Silk Road - A Database and Digital Paleography of Tarim

history.provenance: found in Shorchuk Stadthöhleduring T III Š 72.07

w.n: “to go” part.prs1.nom.sg.m OR gazelle “a tune: 43434 (4x)” m/f.nom.sg
“to restrain, leave behind, restraining, leaving behind” inf.n.subj9b.nom/acc.sg
“(n.) obstruction” “(pp., prep.) apart from, away from, beyond (+ abl.)” “(adv.) apart, separately, into separate pieces, each individually” nom/acc.sg
“(n.) obstruction” “(pp., prep.) apart from, away from, beyond (+ abl.)” “(adv.) apart, separately, into separate pieces, each individually” nom/acc.sg
“to ridicule, cause someone to be ashamed” part.pret3.nom.sg.m
“so, so much, also used as correlative pro-form”
“so, so much, also used as correlative pro-form”
“so, so much, also used as correlative pro-form”
“good, pleasant” “that which is good, a good being” “good, well” gen.pl.m
complementizer occuring in relative clauses (including adverbial clauses)
complementizer occuring in relative clauses (including adverbial clauses)
“(n.) obstruction” “(pp., prep.) apart from, away from, beyond (+ abl.)” “(adv.) apart, separately, into separate pieces, each individually” nom/acc.sg
“(n.) obstruction” “(pp., prep.) apart from, away from, beyond (+ abl.)” “(adv.) apart, separately, into separate pieces, each individually” nom/acc.sg
complementizer occuring in relative clauses (including adverbial clauses)

seg.seg: (to a beauti)ful woman he who loves his life shall not go.
Therefore this one as [she is] affiliated to my relative and mainly in her being beautiful to look at must not be made aware of the love.
Again he thinks:
But who would, having found such a beautiful girl in such a place and at such a time, be able to restrain oneself?

Wiederum denkt er: "Wer könnte aber, wenn er ein so schönes Mädchen an solchem Ort und zu solcher Zeit gefunden hat, sich zurückhalten? Warum sollte ich nicht aus Liebe (zu) ihr ein Gespräch führen? Oder ob ich zuvor nach ihrer Hand fasse?"
Why should I not have a conversation because of love to her?
Or what if I take her by the hand before? (cf 10; 259)
Or what if I take her by the hand before?
When the painter then, full of love, reached for the hand of the artificial girl, this one immediately broke assunder,

Als dann der Maler voll Liebe nach der Hand des künstlichen Mädchens fasste, zerbrach der Mechanismus sofort, auseinander fielen seine Lappen, Schnüre(?) [und] Holzpflöcke(,), und es war kein (Mädchen).
and her rags, ropes [and] pins fell apart,
and there was no longer (a girl).
When the painter saw this, he rose as distraught from his bed and after a regarding it well, (he says):
O dear! I have been terribly deceived/ridiculed by the master mechanic! Oh such [is] the power of passion!
O dear! I have been terribly deceived by the master mechanic! Oh such [is] the power of passion! Oh such [is] the horror of ignorance! If a being loves mere rags so intensely!
Ach die Kraft der Leidenschaft, ach die Macht der Dummheit, wenn ein Mensch schon zu Lappen so heftig Liebe fasst!
Oh what horror/awesome power of ignorance,
when a human being so intensely falls in love with mere rags!
But it is a very true statement of the good/wise ones when they say:
Not the self has been conceived of as the self by the beings.
And in truth there is no self.
|| In the Y.-tune: ||
Just like of the ones composed of rags, pins [and] ropes my imagination was this,
just so of the ones composed of bones, flesh and sinews is the self-imagination of the beings.
If I examine the body parts one by one, there is nothing called “the self”. (cf 11; 260)
If I examine the body parts one by one, there is nothing here called "self".
As my love was towards rags, (just so...)

p.note: Transcription and references have been transferred from the "Text and Reference Database of the Tocharian A Language" (Gerd Carling Lund University) (funded by the Bank of Sweden Tercentenary Foundation and SCAS).

Transcription and translation are based on
.

The translation is largely based on
10-11 , and
.
The tune has 4x18 syllables, rhythm 7/7/4.
ime is a relational noun that takes an adjective in -ṣi and a noun in the locative to mean 'conception of X as Y', cf. b5: tām orṣināṃ śominānaṃ yneś śomiṃṣiṃ ime kälpo.

publicationStmt.publisher: The Characters that shaped the Silk Road - A Database and Digital Paleography of Tarim

history.provenance: found in Shorchuk Stadthöhleduring T III Š 72.08

w.n: demonstrative stem dem.nom.sg.m OR “concerning, in comparison”
“to be” gv.subj5.1sg.subj.act.emph OR demonstrative stem dem.acc.sg.f.emph
“to turn, incline towards” part.pret1.nom/acc.pl.f OR part.pret1.acc.sg.m
“to turn, incline towards” part.pret1.nom/acc.pl.f OR part.pret1.acc.sg.m
“to be” gv.subj5.1sg.subj.act OR demonstrative stem dem.acc.sg.f
“(n.) obstruction” “(pp., prep.) apart from, away from, beyond (+ abl.)” “(adv.) apart, separately, into separate pieces, each individually” nom/acc.sg
“(n.) obstruction” “(pp., prep.) apart from, away from, beyond (+ abl.)” “(adv.) apart, separately, into separate pieces, each individually” nom/acc.sg
“let go, dismiss, emit” gv.pret1.3sg.pst.act OR “pause” m.nom/acc.sg
“(uni.) much, many, very” “(adj.) many” “(n.) multitude, many” nom

seg.seg: (just) so it is to the [living] body in turn. Fie, blind passion!
But this thing has been shown to me by the master mechanic as a skill in his own craft.
Why should I not let him see my own art as well?

Darauf zog der Maler sehr schnell [wtl. schnell schnell] Palette(?) [und] Farben hervor und malte noch in derselben Nacht sich selbst als Getöteten an die Wand mit einem Strick (um) den Hals, an einem Haken(?) hängend.
Then the painter quickly produced a brush(?) and ink(?) and in the same night as one killed, with a rope around his neck, hanging on a nail, he painted himself opposite(?) the door.
But how so? || In the Ṣ.-tune: || The head tilted a little --
the eyes set, with the protruding lips (lit. stretched towards the front) the [last] sighs going out of the throat, hands and feet hanging down,
with the lascivious lower abdomen,
a rope around his neck, hanging on a nail (like one) killed, the golden skin of his body having become pale

... einen Strick um den Hals, an einem Haken(?) hängend als Getöteten, ... [so] sich selbst wie leibhaftig gemalt habend/als er sich wie leibhaftig gemalt hatte, nahm er seine Palette(?) [und] ging hinaus(?).
thus he painted himself as if real, took his painting tools and went under cover.
When in the morning the mechanic came to the painter, he saw the mechanical girl fallen apart
Als dann morgens der Mechaniker zum Maler kam, sah er das künstliche Mädchen auseinandergefallen und sah [auch] den Maler als [Selbst]-getöteten an einem Haken(?) hängend.
and he [also] saw the painter as a self-killed one.
Thereupon he raised his voice as one distraught, being depressed he said:
oh what a pity, what a pity!
Thereupon soon the neighbouring people of the land and much folk gathered and they say:
Who was that? Depressed, the mechanic says: behold, you gentlemen, this my misfortune!
|| In the Ś.-tune: ||

Sehet, [ihr] Herren, dieses mein Unglück! Von den Malermeistern der Malermeister, mein Freund, kam als Gast, mein künstliches Mädchen versah ihm den Dienst. Aus Liebe wohl berührte er das Mädchen, der Mechanismus zerbrach, [und] aus Scham darüber [wtl. aus d[ies]er Scham] tötete sich der Maler selbst, er hängt an einem Haken(?), sehet, [ihr] Guten!
Of the master painters the master of painters,my friend(?), came as a guest, my artificial girl attended to him,

p.note:
Transcription and translation are based on
.

Transcription and references have been transferred from the "Text and Reference Database of the Tocharian A Language" (Gerd Carling Lund University) (funded by the Bank of Sweden Tercentenary Foundation and SCAS).

The translation is largely based on
11-12 , and
.
The tune has a metre of 20-22-10-15 syllables, the rhythm is 5/5/5/5, 8/7/7, 5/5 and 8/7, respectively.

Restoration yre(ki) according to
342-343 after
22, fn. 5 .
The tune has 4x14 syllables, rhythm 7/7.
